title: Effects of marketing and incentives
description: This exercise provides students with an opportunity to practice applying their knowledge of marketing and incentives.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of incentive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a rule that everyone must follow
something that motivates economic agents to act in a certain way
a natural disaster that affects the economy
a new government policy or law that intends to improve working conditions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of consumer sovereignty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the power of consumers to dictate what goods and services are produced based on their purchasing choices
the government's control over what goods and services are available in the market
the ability of companies to set prices for their products without any competition
the influence of international trade on a country's economy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of producer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an individual or organization that uses resources to create goods and services for consumption
an individual or organization that purchases goods and services for personal use
a government entity that regulates the production of goods and services in an economy
a market condition where the supply of goods and services is determined by the availability of resources

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of consumer?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an individual or organization that purchases goods and services for personal use or consumption
organizations and individuals that produce goods and services for the market
an individual that provides labor in exchange for wages
a market condition where the supply of goods and services is determined by the availability of resources

assessment_items.question_content:
A local grocery store decides to offer a discount on fresh fruits and vegetables to encourage healthier eating habits. 

**How are consumers most likely to respond to this incentive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: continue buying the same amount of fresh fruits and vegetables
buy more fresh fruits and vegetables because they are cheaper
stop buying fresh fruits and vegetables altogether
buy less fresh fruits and vegetables because they are on discount

assessment_items.question_content:
A popular smartphone company notices an increase in demand for their new model. In response, they decide to offer a bonus to their workers for each smartphone produced. 

**How are the workers and the company most likely to respond to this incentive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Workers will produce fewer smartphones, and the company will decrease production.
Workers will produce more smartphones, and the company will increase production.
Workers will produce the same number of smartphones, and the company will not change production.
Workers will stop producing smartphones, and the company will stop production.

assessment_items.question_content:
A high school is running a personal finance campaign and wants to encourage students to start saving money. They offer different incentives for students who open a savings account and regularly deposit money.

**Which of the following incentives is most likely to  encourage students to start saving?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a chance to win a scholarship for college
free tickets to a popular local concert for students who save a certain amount each month
a free online course on budgeting and financial planning
monthly cash bonuses added to their savings account based on how much they save

assessment_items.question_content:
A community center is seeking to attract investors to help fund the expansion of its facilities, which serve low-income families, immigrants, and seniors. They offer different incentives to potential investors.

**Which of the following is the best incentive to encourage investors from diverse backgrounds to invest in the community center's expansion?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a promise of a high return on investment within five years
recognition in the local newspaper and a plaque at the community center
tax deductions for the amount invested
opportunities to participate in decision-making for the community center's programs

assessment_items.question_content:
A new beverage company launches a major advertising campaign highlighting the health benefits of their product. 

**What is the most likely impact of this marketing campaign on consumer demand for the beverage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer demand for the beverage will decrease because people will ignore the advertisements.
Consumer demand for the beverage will increase as more people become aware of its health benefits.
Consumer demand for the beverage will stay the same because advertising does not influence consumer behavior.
Consumer demand for the beverage will decrease because the advertisements will make people skeptical.

assessment_items.question_content:
A clothing company notices that consumers are increasingly interested in sustainable and eco-friendly products. In response, the company launched a marketing campaign to highlight their new line of eco-friendly clothing.

**How is the company most likely responding to consumer sovereignty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The company is ignoring consumer preferences and continuing with their original product line.
The company is responding to consumer preferences by promoting products that consumers demand.
The company is reducing the quality of their products to cut costs.
The company is stopping all production of their original product line.

assessment_items.question_content:
A diverse neighborhood is launching programs aimed at high school students. These programs include tutoring, sports, arts, and technology workshops. The center wants to ensure that students from all backgrounds are aware of and excited about these opportunities.

**Which marketing strategy would best help the community center attract a diverse group of students, while respecting consumer sovereignty?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The center should create advertisements that only highlight the technology workshops, as these are likely to be the most popular.

The center should use a variety of advertising channels, including social media, school announcements, and community events. By doing this they will reach students from different backgrounds and emphasize the variety of programs offered.
The center should focus its advertising on the most affluent neighborhoods to ensure they attract students who can afford to participate.
The center should not spend much on advertising and instead rely on word-of-mouth to spread information about the programs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Alexis and Jordan are high school students who want to market their jewelry business to generate more customers. They have a limited marketing budget, but noticed that some established jewelry brands hire social media influencers, while others use flyers and local newspapers.

**Given their limited budget, which marketing strategy should Alexis and Jordan choose to most effectively influence consumer decision making?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: spend all their marketing money on a famous social media influencer to promote their business
create a mix of online and offline advertisements, such as using social media ads and distributing flyers in their local community
focus solely on word-of-mouth by asking their friends and family to spread the word about their business
spend their entire budget on creating a professional website with detailed product descriptions and high-quality images

assessment_items.question_content:
Marketing is an essential part of selling an item in the marketplace. 

**What is the primary role of marketing in business?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to stimulate demand for a product or service
to increase the supply of a product
to make a product cheaper to buy
to make the product blend in with others on the market

assessment_items.question_content:
A local sneaker company, "Step Up," wants to boost demand for their new line of environmentally-friendly sneakers. They decide to collaborate with a popular young athlete known for their commitment to sustainability and social justice.

**Why would Step Up use a celebrity endorsement to increase demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to make their sneakers appear more desirable and trendy to fans of the celebrity
to reduce the production costs of their sneakers
to make the shoes seem more accessible to everyone
to ensure their sneakers are only purchased by the celebrity’s fans

assessment_items.question_content:
A local bookstore, "Readers' Haven," is trying to boost demand for their newly released limited-edition books by creating a sense of urgency. 

**Which of the following strategies would best demonstrate this approach to increase demand?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: announcing that the limited-edition books will only be available for the next two weeks
reducing the prices of all books in the store
partnering with a local charity to donate a portion of their sales
offering a buy-one-get-one-free deal on older book titles

assessment_items.question_content:
"Tech Innovators," a company specializing in smartphones, has just released a new model that boasts improved features over their previous model. They want to increase demand by explaining how the new smartphone is better than the old one. 

**Which of the following strategies would best achieve this goal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: highlighting the new model's longer battery life, faster processor, and enhanced camera quality compared to the old model in their advertising
offering a significant discount on the old model to clear out inventory
partnering with a famous influencer to showcase the old model on social media
providing a trade-in program where customers can exchange their old model for the new one at a reduced price
